tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: Mother and Son Reunited: Vozinha's World Cup Dream Made Complete
stories.summary: After Cape Verde's goalkeeper couldn't afford to bring his mother to his historic match, a U.S. politician and the State Department made their Miami reunion happen.

stories.headline: From Acid Attack Survivor to World Cup Hero: Wissa's Goal for the Ages
stories.summary: Yoane Wissa, who survived a devastating acid attack in 2021, scores a crucial goal for DR Congo, proving resilience conquers even the darkest adversity.

stories.headline: One Lawn Mowing Act Raises $685,000 for Grieving Widow Facing Eviction
stories.summary: Spencer from SB Mowing's viral video helped Debbie, a widow grieving her husband's stage 4 cancer death, raise $685,000 after she fell 3 months behind on rent with no money for food.

stories.headline: Decades of Friendship: Nine-Year-Olds and Ninety-Year-Olds Bond Over Bocce Ball
stories.summary: Grade 4 students from École Tuxedo Park have spent a decade bonding with seniors in their '90s over bocce, celebrating 10 years of the Senior Buddies program in Winnipeg.

stories.headline: Beavers Save the Day: London Wetland Transforms Decade of Flooding Into Natural Wonder
stories.summary: A family of five beavers, extinct in the UK for 400 years, transformed a 24-acre wetland in Ealing and eliminated a decade of flooding in just two years.

stories.headline: After 121 Years of Heartbreak, Belgrano Claims Its First Elite Championship
stories.summary: Substitute Nicolás Fernández scored twice in minutes 85 and 88 to give Belgrano its first-ever elite title after 121 years, defeating River Plate 3-2.

stories.headline: £900 Act of Kindness: Fan Donates World Cup Ticket to Scammed Supporter
stories.summary: John MacLean, 63, gave his £900 World Cup ticket to 23-year-old Jack Rundell after hearing he'd been scammed, sparking a GoFundMe that raised nearly £2,000 for mental health charity.

stories.headline: Great Lakes Piping Plovers Soar to Record 90 Nesting Pairs
stories.summary: Great Lakes piping plovers hit a record 90 nesting pairs this summer, soaring from just 12 pairs in 1990 through decades of dedicated breeding and habitat protection.

stories.headline: Girl Scouts Build a Garden Dream for Woman in Assisted Living
stories.summary: Girl Scouts built a raised garden bed for a woman in assisted living who can no longer tend her own garden.

stories.headline: Ghana's Twenty-Year-Old Stuns Panama With Stoppage-Time World Cup Victory
stories.summary: Twenty-year-old Caleb Yirenkyi scored in stoppage time with a practiced play, giving Ghana a thrilling 1-0 World Cup debut victory over Panama.

stories.headline: Over 700 Endangered Vultures Set to Soar Free Again
stories.summary: Over 700 long-billed vultures bred in captivity by the Bombay Natural History Society are preparing to soar free after being brought back from the brink of extinction.

stories.headline: Solar Desalination Breakthrough: Fresh Water Flows Through the Night
stories.summary: Rice University engineers created a membrane-free solar desalination system that quietly produces fresh drinking water through the night without batteries.

stories.headline: Scientists Restore Aging Immune Cells to Youthful Strength
stories.summary: UCL scientists launched a clinical trial restoring worn-out T cells to youthful function, offering hope for immune rejuvenation and healthier aging.
